title: Class 12 Physics — Competitive Quiz Bank
subject: Physics
copyrightDisclaimer: © IIS Academy supplemental quiz content. Use strictly for educational purposes.

questions.question: The SI unit of electric permittivity ε₀ is:

feedback.correct: Correct! ε₀ = 8.85×10⁻¹² C²/(N·m²). It appears in Coulomb's constant k = 1/(4πε₀).
feedback.incorrect: ε₀ is the permittivity of free space. From Coulomb's Law F = q₁q₂/(4πε₀r²), the unit of ε₀ = C²/(N·m²).
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: ε₀ also appears in the energy density of electric fields (u = ε₀E²/2) — critical for capacitor energy storage in EV batteries.

questions.question: Which law states that the algebraic sum of all voltages around a closed loop is zero?

feedback.correct: Correct! Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL): ΣV = 0 around any closed loop — based on conservation of energy.
feedback.incorrect: Kirchhoff's Voltage Law (KVL) states ΣV = 0 around a closed loop. KCL states that ΣI = 0 at any node.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: KVL is used to design every circuit board — from Arduino microcontrollers to the chips inside your phone. Circuit simulation software (SPICE) uses KVL millions of times per second.

questions.question: An electric dipole of moment p is placed at origin along x-axis. The ratio of electric field at axial point (2a, 0) to equatorial point (0, 2a) is:

feedback.correct: Correct! E_axial = 2kp/r³ and E_equatorial = kp/r³. So ratio = 2:1. This is a classic JEE pattern.
feedback.incorrect: E_axial = 2kp/r³ (along dipole direction). E_equatorial = kp/r³ (opposite to dipole). Ratio = 2:1 at same distance r.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: Molecular dipoles (water, HCl) are fundamental to how solvents interact with drug molecules. Drug design uses this 2:1 ratio effect to understand molecular interactions.

questions.question: A circular coil of 100 turns, area 10 cm², is placed in a uniform magnetic field B = 1T. The field reverses completely in 0.01 s. The average EMF induced is:

feedback.correct: Correct! ΔΦ = NBAcos0° − NBA cos180° = 2NBA = 2×100×1×10×10⁻⁴ = 0.2 Wb. EMF = ΔΦ/Δt = 0.2/0.01 = 20V? Wait — recalculate: 2×100×1×10⁻³ = 0.2 Wb. EMF = 0.2/0.01 = 20V. Actually answer A is 20V.
feedback.incorrect: ΔΦ = 2NBA (field reverses = change is 2×initial flux). EMF = NΔΦ/Δt... careful with units.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: This principle is used in regenerative braking in EVs — the motor runs in reverse as a generator when braking, converting kinetic energy back to electrical energy.

questions.question: In a transistor used as a digital switch in a logic gate, when the base current is zero, the transistor is in:
options: Saturation region — acts as closed switch
Cut-off region — acts as open switch

feedback.correct: Correct! Zero base current → transistor cut-off → no collector current → output HIGH in CMOS logic. This is the 'off' state of every digital switch in every chip.
feedback.incorrect: When I_B = 0: transistor is cut-off (no I_C), acting as an open switch. In saturation (I_B maximum), it acts as a closed switch.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: The transistor you study in Class 12 is the fundamental building block of ALL computers. Intel's latest processors pack 20 billion transistors on a chip smaller than a fingernail — all operating on this cut-off/saturation principle.

questions.question: A solar panel converts sunlight to electricity using the photoelectric effect. If the frequency of blue light is 6.7×10¹⁴ Hz and work function of silicon is 1.1 eV, what is the kinetic energy of emitted electrons?

feedback.incorrect: KE = hf − φ (Einstein's photoelectric equation). hf = 6.626×10⁻³⁴ × 6.7×10¹⁴ J ÷ 1.6×10⁻¹⁹ = 2.78 eV. KE = 2.78 − 1.1 = 1.68 eV.
feedback.intelligenceAgeHint: This calculation determines the efficiency of every solar cell. Engineers design solar panels to match photon energies to semiconductor work functions — maximizing the kinetic energy of electrons and thus the electrical output. India's target: 500 GW solar by 2030.
